summary: First-finish smoke test of IT Help Desk. Auth, users CRUD, ticket lifecycle (create, assign, status update, history), comments (with internal-note filtering for requesters), SLA defaults, KB CRUD+search+feedback, notifications, dashboard stats, password reset request, and frontend login + tickets queue + dashboard charts all working. One HIGH-priority backend bug: GET /api/tickets/export.csv returns 500 due to route ordering ({id} matches before export.csv).
passed: POST /api/auth/login (admin seed) returns user + JWT token
GET /api/auth/me with Bearer token returns admin user
POST /api/users creates technician and requester (admin only)
POST /api/tickets creates TCK-0001 with ticket_number, requester populated
PATCH /api/tickets/{id} updates assignee + status to Assigned, returns enriched assignee object
POST /api/tickets/{id}/comments with internal=true accepts; GET comments as requester returns [] (internal hidden) - filter works
Notification 'You have been assigned ticket TCK-0001' created for technician (GET /api/notifications)
GET /api/sla returns default rules for Critical/High/Medium/Low
POST /api/kb (with category) creates article; GET /api/kb?search returns it; POST /api/kb/{id}/feedback?helpful=true ok
Frontend login redirects to /tickets, sidebar renders (Dashboard/Tickets/KB/Users/Settings), TCK-0001 row visible with assignee Tech1
Dashboard renders Recharts SVGs (status bar, 14d timeline line, category pie, priority, tech workload)
JWT via Authorization Bearer header works end-to-end (token persisted in localStorage)

failed.issue: GET /api/tickets/export.csv returns HTTP 500. Backend log: 'bson.errors.InvalidId: \'export.csv\' is not a valid ObjectId'. The dynamic route GET /api/tickets/{id} is declared before/matching /api/tickets/export.csv, so 'export.csv' is parsed as the id parameter.
failed.evidence: curl -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' /api/tickets/export.csv -> 500; in-browser fetch from authenticated session also returns 500. Backend log shows InvalidId traceback. Frontend has an 'Export CSV' button on /tickets that will fail.

failed.issue: POST /api/kb requires 'category' field but it is not optional/documented in problem statement examples. Not a bug per se but worth confirming UI sends it. (UI not exercised yet in this run for KB editor)
failed.evidence: POST {title,body,tags} -> 422 'Field required: category'. With category='Account Access' the request succeeds.

action_items: Fix route ordering in /app/backend/server.py: move @app.get('/api/tickets/export.csv') ABOVE @app.get('/api/tickets/{id}') (or rename to /api/tickets/export so it doesn't collide). Then re-verify Export CSV downloads from the Tickets page.
Confirm KbEditor on the frontend always sends 'category' to /api/kb. Optional: make category default server-side.

mocked_apis: Resend email (forgot-password) - placeholder API key, emails logged to console
untested_in_this_run: POST /api/auth/reset-password end-to-end (needs to fetch token from MongoDB)
Frontend flows: CreateTicket form, KB editor, Users management, Settings/SLA update, notification badge interaction
